lex: tail overview recursion optimization, tail call optimization tco, recursive function stack overflow
vec: what is tail recursion and how does tail call optimization prevent stack overflow
hyde: Tail recursion: recursive call is the last operation, no work after it returns. TCO reuses stack frame instead of adding new one—prevents stack overflow. Convert by passing accumulated result as parameter: factorial(n, acc=1) { return n <= 1 ? acc : factorial(n-1, n*acc); }. Not all languages implement TCO—JavaScript in strict mode, Scheme yes, Python no.
